```text
    88→
    89→function _shouldHandleBusyConflict(args) {
    90→  try {
    91→    const rawUrl = typeof args[0] === 'string' ? args[0] : args[0]?.url;
    92→    if (!rawUrl) return true;
    93→    const url = new URL(rawUrl, window.location.origin);
    94→    return url.pathname !== '/api/sessions/claim' && url.pathname !== '/api/sessions/takeover';
    95→  } catch (_) {
    96→    return true;
    97→  }
    98→}
    99→
   100→(function() {
   101→  const _origFetch = window.fetch;
   102→  window.fetch = async function(...args) {
   103→    const resp = await _origFetch.apply(this, args);
   104→    if (resp.status === 409 && _shouldHandleBusyConflict(args)) {
   105→      showSessionConflictBanner();
   106→      const shouldRetry = await new Promise(resolve => _conflictRetryQueue.push(resolve));
   107→      if (shouldRetry) return _origFetch.apply(this, args);
   108→    }
   109→    return resp;
   110→  };
   111→})();
   112→
   113→async function createNewSessionAndNavigate() {
   114→  const data = await createSession();
   115→  if (!data.session_id) throw new Error('Failed to create session');
   116→  window.location.assign(data.redirect_url || `/?session=${data.session_id}`);
   117→}
   118→
   119→async function createNewSessionInNewTab() {
   120→  const data = await createSession();
   121→  if (!data.session_id) throw new Error('Failed to create session');
   122→  window.open(data.redirect_url || `/?session=${data.session_id}`, '_blank', 'noopener');
   123→}
   124→
   125→function formatSessionPhaseLabel(phase) {
   126→  if (!phase) return 'init';
   127→  return SESSION_PHASE_LABELS[phase] || String(phase).replace(/_/g, ' ');
   128→}
   129→
   130→function _getCurrentSessionIdValue() {
   131→  if (typeof getSessionIdFromURL === 'function') {
   132→    return getSessionIdFromURL();
   133→  }
   134→  try {
   135→    return new URLSearchParams(window.location.search).get('session');
   136→  } catch (_) {
   137→    return null;
   138→  }
   139→}
   140→
   141→function _getCurrentOwnerTokenValue() {
   142→  if (typeof getOwnerToken === 'function') {
   143→    return getOwnerToken();
   144→  }
   145→  try {
   146→    return sessionStorage.getItem('cv-builder-owner-token');
   147→  } catch (_) {
   148→    return null;
   149→  }
   150→}
   151→
   152→function buildSessionSwitcherLabel(status = {}) {
   153→  const positionName = (status.position_name || '').toString().trim();
   154→  const phase = formatSessionPhaseLabel(status.phase);
   155→  if (positionName) {
   156→    return `${positionName} · ${phase}`;
   157→  }
   158→  return _getCurrentSessionIdValue() ? `Session · ${phase}` : '📂 Sessions';
   159→}
   160→
   161→function getActiveSessionOwnershipMeta(session, {
   162→  currentSessionId = _getCurrentSessionIdValue(),
   163→  currentOwnerToken = _getCurrentOwnerTokenValue(),
   164→} = {}) {
   165→  if (!session || typeof session !== 'object') {
   166→    return { label: 'Unknown', className: 'session-status-unclaimed', isCurrent: false };
   167→  }
   168→
   169→  const isCurrentSession = Boolean(currentSessionId) && session.session_id === currentSessionId;
   170→  const sameOwner = Boolean(currentOwnerToken) && session.owner_token === currentOwnerToken;
   171→
   172→  if (isCurrentSession && sameOwner) {
   173→    return { label: 'Current tab', className: 'session-status-current', isCurrent: true };
   174→  }
   175→  if (sameOwner) {
   176→    return { label: 'Owned by this tab', className: 'session-status-current', isCurrent: false };
   177→  }
   178→  if (session.owner_token) {
   179→    return { label: 'Owned by another tab', className: 'session-status-owned', isCurrent: false };
   180→  }
   181→  return { label: 'Unclaimed', className: 'session-status-unclaimed', isCurrent: false };
   182→}
   183→
   184→function formatSessionTimestamp(timestamp, { includeTime = true } = {}) {
   185→  if (!timestamp) return '—';
   186→  try {
   187→    return new Date(timestamp).toLocaleString('en-US', {
   188→      month: 'short', day: 'numeric', year: 'numeric',
   189→      ...(includeTime ? { hour: 'numeric', minute: '2-digit' } : {}),
   190→    });
   191→  } catch (_) {
   192→    return String(timestamp).replace('T', ' ').slice(0, includeTime ? 16 : 10);
   193→  }
   194→}
   195→
   196→function _renderActiveSessionRows(activeSessions) {
   197→  if (!activeSessions.length) {
   198→    return '<p class="session-switcher-empty">No active in-memory sessions.</p>';
   199→  }
   200→
   201→  const currentSessionId = _getCurrentSessionIdValue();
   202→  const currentOwnerToken = _getCurrentOwnerTokenValue();
   203→
   204→  return `<div class="session-switcher-list">${activeSessions.map(session => {
   205→    const ownership = getActiveSessionOwnershipMeta(session, { currentSessionId, currentOwnerToken });
   206→    const openHref = `/?session=${encodeURIComponent(session.session_id || '')}`;
   207→    const actionHtml = ownership.isCurrent
   208→      ? '<span class="session-switcher-btn" aria-disabled="true">Current</span>'
   209→      : `<a class="session-switcher-link" href="${openHref}">Open</a>`;
   210→    return `
   211→      <div class="session-switcher-row">
   212→        <div class="session-switcher-row-main">
   213→          <div class="session-switcher-row-title">
   214→            <strong>${escapeHtml(session.position_name || 'Untitled')}</strong>
   215→            <span class="session-status-pill ${ownership.className}"><span class="session-status-dot"></span>${escapeHtml(ownership.label)}</span>
   216→          </div>
   217→          <div class="session-switcher-row-meta">
   218→            ${escapeHtml(formatSessionPhaseLabel(session.phase))} · Created ${escapeHtml(formatSessionTimestamp(session.created))} · Last modified ${escapeHtml(formatSessionTimestamp(session.last_modified))}
   219→          </div>
   220→        </div>
   221→        <div class="session-switcher-actions">${actionHtml}</div>
   222→      </div>`;
   223→  }).join('')}</div>`;
   224→}
   225→
   226→function _renderSavedSessionRows(savedSessions, { includeManagement = false } = {}) {
   227→  if (!savedSessions.length) {
   228→    return '<p class="session-switcher-empty">No saved sessions found.</p>';
   229→  }
   230→
   231→  return `<div class="session-switcher-list">${savedSessions.map((session, index) => {
   232→    const escapedPath = escapeHtml(session.path || '');
   233→    const managementHtml = includeManagement
   234→      ? `
   235→        <button data-sm-action="rename" data-sm-path="${escapedPath}" data-sm-idx="${index}" class="session-switcher-btn" title="Rename session">Rename</button>
   236→        <button data-sm-action="delete" data-sm-path="${escapedPath}" class="session-switcher-btn danger" title="Delete session">Delete</button>`
   237→      : '';
   238→
   239→    return `
   240→      <div class="session-switcher-row">
   241→        <div class="session-switcher-row-main">
   242→          <div class="session-switcher-row-title">
   243→            <strong id="sm-name-${index}">${escapeHtml(session.position_name || 'Untitled')}</strong>
   244→            <span class="session-status-pill session-status-saved"><span class="session-status-dot"></span>Saved</span>
   245→          </div>
   246→          <div id="sm-rename-${index}" style="display:none;align-items:center;gap:6px;margin:8px 0 4px;">
   247→            <input id="sm-input-${index}" type="text" value="${escapeHtml(session.position_name || '')}" class="sm-key-input" data-sm-path="${escapedPath}" data-sm-idx="${index}" style="border:1px solid #3b82f6;border-radius:6px;padding:6px 10px;font-size:13px;flex:1;min-width:0;">
   248→            <button data-sm-action="submit-rename" data-sm-path="${escapedPath}" data-sm-idx="${index}" class="session-switcher-btn">Save</button>
   249→            <button data-sm-action="cancel-rename" data-sm-idx="${index}" class="session-switcher-btn">Cancel</button>
   250→          </div>
   251→          <div class="session-switcher-row-meta">
   252→            ${escapeHtml(formatSessionPhaseLabel(session.phase))} · Saved ${escapeHtml(formatSessionTimestamp(session.timestamp))}
   253→          </div>
   254→        </div>
   255→        <div class="session-switcher-actions">
   256→          <button data-sm-action="load" data-sm-path="${escapedPath}" class="session-switcher-link">Load</button>
   257→          ${managementHtml}
   258→        </div>
   259→      </div>`;
   260→  }).join('')}</div>`;
   261→}
   262→
   263→function _renderSessionSwitcherSections(activeSessions, savedSessions, { includeSavedManagement = false } = {}) {
   264→  const savedSectionNote = includeSavedManagement
   265→    ? 'Load from disk, rename, or delete saved work.'
   266→    : 'Load saved work from disk.';
   267→
   268→  return `
   269→    <div class="session-switcher-sections">
   270→      <section class="session-switcher-section">
   271→        <div class="session-switcher-section-header">
   272→          <h3>Active Sessions</h3>
   273→          <span class="session-switcher-section-note">In-memory sessions that can be opened directly.</span>
   274→        </div>
   275→        ${_renderActiveSessionRows(activeSessions)}
   276→      </section>
   277→      <section class="session-switcher-section">
   278→        <div class="session-switcher-section-header">
   279→          <h3>Saved Sessions</h3>
   280→          <span class="session-switcher-section-note">${savedSectionNote}</span>
   281→        </div>
   282→        ${_renderSavedSessionRows(savedSessions, { includeManagement: includeSavedManagement })}
   283→      </section>
   284→    </div>`;
   285→}
   286→
   287→function _updateSessionSwitcherHeader(status = {}) {
   288→  const label = buildSessionSwitcherLabel(status);
   289→  const switcherLabelEl = document.getElementById('session-switcher-label');
   290→  const switcherBtn = document.getElementById('session-switcher-btn');
   291→  const subtitleEl = document.getElementById('header-session-name');
   292→  const hasSession = Boolean(_getCurrentSessionIdValue());
   293→
   294→  if (switcherLabelEl) switcherLabelEl.textContent = label;
   295→  if (switcherBtn) switcherBtn.classList.toggle('is-session-active', hasSession);
   296→  if (subtitleEl) {
   297→    subtitleEl.textContent = hasSession ? `Current session: ${label}` : 'Select or create a session';
   298→  }
   299→}
   300→
   301→function showOwnershipConflictDialog(message = 'This session is currently claimed by another browser tab.') {
   302→  return new Promise(resolve => {
   303→    const overlay = document.getElementById('ownership-conflict-overlay');
   304→    const messageEl = document.getElementById('ownership-conflict-message');
   305→    const loadDifferentBtn = document.getElementById('ownership-load-different-btn');
   306→    const newSessionBtn = document.getElementById('ownership-new-session-btn');
   307→    const takeoverBtn = document.getElementById('ownership-takeover-btn');
   308→    if (!overlay || !messageEl || !loadDifferentBtn || !newSessionBtn || !takeoverBtn) {
   309→      resolve('different');
   310→      return;
   311→    }
   312→
   313→    const cleanup = (choice) => {
   314→      overlay.style.display = 'none';
   315→      restoreFocus();
   316→      loadDifferentBtn.onclick = null;
   317→      newSessionBtn.onclick = null;
   318→      takeoverBtn.onclick = null;
   319→      resolve(choice);
   320→    };
   321→
   322→    messageEl.textContent = message;
   323→    overlay.style.display = 'flex';
   324→    _focusedElementBeforeModal = document.activeElement;
   325→    setInitialFocus('ownership-conflict-overlay');
   326→    trapFocus('ownership-conflict-overlay');
   327→
   328→    loadDifferentBtn.onclick = () => cleanup('different');
   329→    newSessionBtn.onclick = () => cleanup('new');
   330→    takeoverBtn.onclick = () => cleanup('takeover');
   331→  });
   332→}
   333→
   334→function closeOwnershipConflictDialog(choice = 'different') {
   335→  const overlay = document.getElementById('ownership-conflict-overlay');
   336→  if (overlay) overlay.style.display = 'none';
   337→  restoreFocus();
   338→  const loadDifferentBtn = document.getElementById('ownership-load-different-btn');
   339→  if (loadDifferentBtn && typeof loadDifferentBtn.onclick === 'function') {
   340→    loadDifferentBtn.onclick();
   341→    return;
   342→  }
   343→  return choice;
   344→}
   345→
   346→async function _claimCurrentSession(sessionIdToClaim) {
   347→  const res = await fetch('/api/sessions/claim', {
   348→    method: 'POST',
   349→    headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
   350→    body: JSON.stringify({
   351→      session_id: sessionIdToClaim,
   352→      owner_token: getOwnerToken(),
   353→    }),
   354→  });
   355→
   356→  if (res.ok) return true;
   357→
   358→  const data = await res.json().catch(() => ({}));
   359→  if (res.status === 409 && data.error === 'session_owned') {
   360→    const action = await showOwnershipConflictDialog(
   361→      'This session is currently claimed by another browser tab. You can take it over, start a new session, or load a different one.'
   362→    );
   363→    if (action === 'new') {
   364→      await createNewSessionAndNavigate();
   365→      return false;
   366→    }
   367→    if (action !== 'takeover') {
   368→      showSessionsLandingPanel('Select a different session or create a new one.');
   369→      openSessionsModal();
   370→      return false;
   371→    }
   372→
   373→    const takeoverRes = await fetch('/api/sessions/takeover', {
   374→      method: 'POST',
   375→      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
   376→      body: JSON.stringify({
   377→        session_id: sessionIdToClaim,
   378→        owner_token: getOwnerToken(),
   379→      }),
   380→    });
   381→    const takeoverData = await takeoverRes.json().catch(() => ({}));
   382→    if (!takeoverRes.ok) {
   383→      throw new Error(takeoverData.error || 'Failed to take over session');
   384→    }
   385→    return true;
   386→  }
   387→
   388→  if (res.status === 404) {
   389→    showSessionsLandingPanel('That session is no longer active. Load it from disk or create a new one.');
   390→    openSessionsModal();
   391→    return false;
   392→  }
   393→
   394→  throw new Error(data.error || `Failed to claim session (${res.status})`);
   395→}
   396→
   397→async function openSavedSessionFromLanding(path) {
   398→  try {
   399→    await loadSessionFile(path);
   400→  } catch (error) {
   401→    appendMessage('system', `❌ Could not load session: ${error.message}`);
   402→  }
   403→}
   404→
   405→function openActiveSessionFromLanding(sessionIdToOpen) {
   406→  if (!sessionIdToOpen) return;
   407→  window.location.assign(`/?session=${encodeURIComponent(sessionIdToOpen)}`);
   408→}
   409→
   410→function showSessionsLandingPanel(message = '') {
   411→  currentTab = 'job';
   412→  currentStage = 'job';
   413→  updateActionButtons('job');
   414→  updatePositionTitle({});
   415→
   416→  const content = document.getElementById('document-content');
   417→  if (!content) return;
   418→
   419→  content.innerHTML = `
   420→    <div class="session-switcher-landing-shell">
   421→      <div class="session-switcher-landing-header">
   422→        <div class="session-switcher-landing-copy">
   423→          <h2>Select a Session</h2>
   424→          <p>Each browser tab now works against its own URL-scoped session.</p>
   425→        </div>
   426→        <div class="session-switcher-landing-actions">
   427→          <button class="action-btn" onclick="createNewSessionInNewTab()">＋ New Session in New Tab</button>
   428→        </div>
   429→      </div>
   430→      ${message ? `<p style="margin-bottom:20px;color:#b45309;font-weight:600;">${escapeHtml(message)}</p>` : ''}
   431→      <div style="display:flex;gap:12px;margin-bottom:24px;flex-wrap:wrap;">
   432→        <button class="action-btn primary" onclick="createNewSessionAndNavigate()">+ New Session</button>
   433→      </div>
   434→    </div>
   435→  `;
   436→}
   437→
   438→async function ensureSessionContext() {
   439→  const urlSessionId = getSessionIdFromURL();
   440→  if (!urlSessionId) {
   441→    showSessionsLandingPanel();
   442→    openSessionsModal();
   443→    return false;
   444→  }
   445→
   446→  sessionId = urlSessionId;
   447→  localStorage.setItem(StorageKeys.SESSION_ID, sessionId);
   448→  return _claimCurrentSession(urlSessionId);
   449→}
   450→
   451→async function restoreSession() {
   452→  try {
   453→    isReconnecting = true;
   454→
   455→    const storedSessionId = getSessionIdFromURL() || localStorage.getItem(StorageKeys.SESSION_ID);
   456→    if (!storedSessionId) {
   457→      isReconnecting = false;
   458→      return;
   459→    }
   460→    sessionId = storedSessionId;
   461→    localStorage.setItem(StorageKeys.SESSION_ID, storedSessionId);
   462→    
   463→    // Try to restore conversation history from backend
   464→    const historyRes = await fetch('/api/history');
   465→    if (historyRes.ok) {
   466→      const historyData = await historyRes.json();
   467→      
   468→      // Always restore from the server session file (authoritative history source).
   469→      if (historyData.history && historyData.history.length > 0) {
   470→        const conversation = document.getElementById('conversation');
   471→        conversation.innerHTML = ''; // Clear any loading messages
   472→        
   473→        historyData.history.forEach(msg => {
   474→          if (msg.role === 'user') {
   475→            appendMessage('user', msg.content);
   476→          } else if (msg.role === 'assistant') {
   477→            appendMessage('assistant', msg.content);
   478→          }
   479→        });
   480→        
   481→        appendMessage('system', '🔄 Session restored from server.');
   482→        console.log(`Restored ${historyData.history.length} messages from backend`);
   483→      }
   484→      
   485→      // Update phase
   486→      if (historyData.phase) {
   487→        lastKnownPhase = historyData.phase;
   488→      }
   489→    }
   490→    
   491→    // Try to restore backend state.
   492→    // Returns true when server had live session data (in memory or loaded from disk).
   493→    const serverHasData = await restoreBackendState();
   494→
   495→    // Restore UI-only prefs (activeReviewPane) from localStorage.
   496→    // When the server already has core data, skip restoring tabData/interactiveState/
   497→    // pendingRecommendations from localStorage — server is authoritative for those.
   498→    restoreTabData({ uiPrefsOnly: serverHasData });
   499→
   500→    isReconnecting = false;
   501→    
   502→  } catch (error) {
   503→    console.warn('Session restoration failed:', error);
   504→    appendMessage('system', `⚠️ Could not restore previous session. Starting fresh. (${error.message})`);
   505→    isReconnecting = false;
   506→  }
   507→}
   508→
   509→async function restoreBackendState() {
   510→  // Returns true if the server had any live session data (in memory or loaded from disk).
   511→  // Callers use this to decide whether localStorage core fields should be skipped.
   512→  try {
   513→    const statusRes = await fetch('/api/status');
   514→    if (!statusRes.ok) return false;
   515→    const statusData = parseStatusResponse(await statusRes.json());
   516→
   517→    let serverHasData = false;
   518→
   519→    // If backend has live state, restore it into tabData
   520→    if (statusData.job_analysis) {
   521→      tabData.analysis = statusData.job_analysis;
   522→      serverHasData = true;
   523→      console.log('Restored analysis data from backend memory');
   524→    }
   525→    if (statusData.customizations) {
   526→      tabData.customizations = statusData.customizations;
   527→      window.pendingRecommendations = statusData.customizations;
   528→      serverHasData = true;
   529→      console.log('Restored customizations data from backend memory');
   530→    }
   531→    if (statusData.generated_files) {
   532→      tabData.cv = statusData.generated_files;
   533→      serverHasData = true;
   534→      console.log('Restored CV data from backend memory');
   535→    }
   536→
   537→    // If backend has no active session (cold start after server restart),
   538→    // try to auto-load the most recent session from disk.
   539→    if (!statusData.position_name && !statusData.job_analysis) {
   540→      const storedPath = localStorage.getItem(StorageKeys.SESSION_PATH);
   541→      if (storedPath) {
   542→        const loaded = await loadSessionFile(storedPath);
   543→        if (loaded) return true;
   544→      }
   545→    }
   546→
   547→    return serverHasData;
   548→  } catch (error) {
   549→    console.warn('Failed to restore backend state:', error);
   550→    return false;
   551→  }
   552→}
   553→
   554→async function loadSessionFile(path) {
   555→  try {
   556→    appendMessage('system', '🔄 Restoring session from file...');
   557→    const res = await fetch('/api/load-session', {
   558→      method: 'POST',
   559→      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
   560→      body: JSON.stringify({ path })
   561→    });
   562→    if (!res.ok) {
   563→      const err = await res.json();
   564→      appendMessage('system', `❌ Failed to restore session: ${err.error}`);
   565→      return false;
   566→    }
   567→    const data = await res.json();
   568→
   569→    if (data.redirect_url && getSessionIdFromURL() !== data.session_id) {
   570→      window.location.assign(data.redirect_url);
   571→      return true;
   572→    }
   573→
   574→    // Persist the loaded path so auto-reload can find it after the next server restart
   575→    localStorage.setItem(StorageKeys.SESSION_PATH, data.session_file || path);
   576→
   577→    // Reload conversation history and status from the freshly-loaded backend
   578→    const historyRes = await fetch('/api/history');
   579→    if (historyRes.ok) {
   580→      const historyData = await historyRes.json();
   581→      const conv = document.getElementById('conversation');
   582→      conv.innerHTML = '';
   583→      (historyData.history || []).forEach(msg => {
   584→        if (msg.role !== 'system') appendMessage(msg.role, msg.content);
   585→      });
   586→    } else {
   587→      appendMessage('system', '⚠ Could not restore conversation history.');
   588→    }
   589→
   590→    await fetchStatus();
   591→
   592→    // Rehydrate tabData and switch to the correct tab for the restored phase
   593→    const sessionPhase = data.phase || PHASES.INIT;
   594→    const phaseTabMap = {
   595→      [PHASES.INIT]:           'job',
   596→      [PHASES.JOB_ANALYSIS]:   'analysis',
   597→      [PHASES.CUSTOMIZATION]:  'exp-review',
   598→      [PHASES.REWRITE_REVIEW]: 'rewrite',
   599→      [PHASES.SPELL_CHECK]:    'spell',
   600→      [PHASES.GENERATION]:     'generate',
   601→      [PHASES.LAYOUT_REVIEW]:  'layout',
   602→      [PHASES.REFINEMENT]:     'finalise',
   603→    };
   604→    const targetTab = phaseTabMap[sessionPhase] || 'job';
   605→
   606→    // For phases that need customizations data, rehydrate from status
   607→    const customizationPhases = [
   608→      PHASES.CUSTOMIZATION, PHASES.REWRITE_REVIEW, PHASES.SPELL_CHECK,
   609→      PHASES.GENERATION, PHASES.LAYOUT_REVIEW, PHASES.REFINEMENT,
   610→    ];
   611→    if (customizationPhases.includes(sessionPhase)) {
   612→      try {
   613→        const s2 = await fetch('/api/status');
   614→        const sd = await s2.json();
   615→        if (sd.customizations) {
   616→          tabData.customizations = sd.customizations;
   617→          window.pendingRecommendations = sd.customizations;
   618→        }
   619→        if (sd.job_analysis) tabData.analysis = sd.job_analysis;
   620→        if (sd.generated_files) tabData.cv = sd.generated_files;
   621→        // Restore achievement edits so the editor shows session-specific bullets, not master CV
   622→        if (sd.achievement_edits && Object.keys(sd.achievement_edits).length > 0) {
   623→          window.achievementEdits = {};
   624→          for (const [k, v] of Object.entries(sd.achievement_edits)) {
   625→            window.achievementEdits[parseInt(k, 10)] = Array.isArray(v) ? v : [v];
   626→          }
   627→        }
   628→      } catch (_) { /* non-fatal */ }
   629→    }
   630→
   631→    // For rewrite_review phase, pre-populate the rewrite panel cache so the Rewrites tab renders
   632→    if (sessionPhase === PHASES.REWRITE_REVIEW) {
   633→      try {
   634→        const rr = await fetch('/api/rewrites');
   635→        if (rr.ok) {
   636→          const rd = parseRewritesResponse(await rr.json());
   637→          const rewrites = rd.rewrites || [];
   638→          const warnings = rd.persuasion_warnings || [];
   639→          if (rewrites.length > 0) {
   640→            rewriteDecisions = {};
   641→            renderRewritePanel(rewrites, warnings);
   642→          }
   643→        }
   644→      } catch (_) { /* non-fatal */ }
   645→    }
   646→
   647→    switchTab(targetTab);
   648→    appendMessage('system', `✅ Session restored: ${data.position_name || 'Unnamed'} (${data.phase || PHASES.INIT})`);
   649→    return true;
   650→  } catch (err) {
   651→    appendMessage('system', `❌ Error restoring session: ${err.message}`);
   652→    return false;
   653→  }
   654→}
   655→
   656→// ── LLM abort helper ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   657→// Wraps fetch() and automatically attaches the current AbortController signal.
   658→function llmFetch(url, options = {}) {
   659→  if (window._currentAbortController) {
   660→    options.signal = window._currentAbortController.signal;
   661→  }
   662→  return fetch(url, options);
   663→}
   664→
   665→function abortCurrentRequest() {
   666→  if (window._currentAbortController) {
   667→    window._currentAbortController.abort();
   668→    window._currentAbortController = null;
   669→    setLoading(false);
   670→    appendMessage('system', '⏹ Request stopped.');
   671→  }
   672→}
   673→
   674→// ── LLM status bar ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   675→let _llmElapsedTimer = null;
   676→let _llmStartTime    = null;
   677→
   678→function _updateLLMStatusBar(loading, label) {
   679→  const bar       = document.getElementById('llm-status-bar');
   680→  const thinking  = document.getElementById('llm-thinking');
   681→  const abortBtn  = document.getElementById('llm-abort-btn');
   682→  const stepLabel = document.getElementById('llm-step-label');
   683→  const elapsedEl = document.getElementById('llm-elapsed');
   684→  if (!bar) return;
   685→  if (loading) {
   686→    bar.style.display    = 'flex';
   687→    if (thinking) thinking.style.display = 'flex';
   688→    if (abortBtn) abortBtn.style.display  = '';
   689→    if (stepLabel) stepLabel.textContent  = label || 'Reasoning…';
   690→    if (elapsedEl) elapsedEl.textContent  = '';
   691→    // Start elapsed-time ticker
   692→    _llmStartTime = Date.now();
   693→    clearInterval(_llmElapsedTimer);
   694→    _llmElapsedTimer = setInterval(() => {
   695→      if (!elapsedEl) return;
   696→      const secs = Math.floor((Date.now() - _llmStartTime) / 1000);
   697→      if (secs >= 3) {
   698→        elapsedEl.textContent = ` · ${secs}s`;
   699→      }
   700→    }, 1000);
   701→  } else {
   702→    clearInterval(_llmElapsedTimer);
   703→    _llmElapsedTimer = null;
   704→    _llmStartTime    = null;
   705→    if (thinking) thinking.style.display = 'none';
   706→    if (abortBtn) abortBtn.style.display  = 'none';
   707→    if (stepLabel) stepLabel.textContent  = 'Reasoning…';
   708→    if (elapsedEl) elapsedEl.textContent  = '';
   709→    // Refresh token count (non-blocking); hides bar if fetch fails
   710→    _refreshContextStats();
   711→  }
   712→}
   713→
   714→async function _refreshContextStats() {
   715→  const bar      = document.getElementById('llm-status-bar');
   716→  const tokenEl  = document.getElementById('llm-token-count');
   717→  if (!bar || !tokenEl) return;
   718→  try {
   719→    const res  = await fetch('/api/context-stats');
   720→    if (!res.ok) return;
   721→    const data = await res.json();
   722→    if (!data.ok) return;
   723→    const est    = data.estimated_tokens || 0;
   724→    const win    = data.context_window   || 128_000;
   725→    const exact  = data.token_source === 'exact';
   726→    const pct    = Math.round((est / win) * 100);
   727→    const estStr = est >= 1000 ? `${(est / 1000).toFixed(1)}K` : `${est}`;
   728→    const winStr = win >= 1000 ? `${Math.round(win / 1000)}K`  : `${win}`;
   729→    tokenEl.textContent  = `${exact ? '' : '~'}${estStr} / ${winStr} (${pct}%)`;
   730→    bar.style.display    = 'flex';
   731→  } catch (_) { /* silently ignore */ }
   732→}
   733→
   734→// ── Sessions modal ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   735→async function openSessionsModal() {
   736→  const overlay = document.getElementById('sessions-modal-overlay');
   737→  if (!overlay) return;
   738→  overlay.style.display = 'flex';
   739→  _focusedElementBeforeModal = document.activeElement;
   740→  await _renderSessionsModalBody();
   741→  _refreshTrashBadge();
   742→  trapFocus('sessions-modal-overlay');
   743→}
   744→
   745→function closeSessionsModal() {
   746→  const overlay = document.getElementById('sessions-modal-overlay');
   747→  if (overlay) overlay.style.display = 'none';
   748→  restoreFocus();
   749→}
   750→
   751→async function _renderSessionsModalBody() {
   752→  const body = document.getElementById('sessions-modal-body');
   753→  if (!body) return;
   754→  body.innerHTML = '<div style="padding:24px;display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center;gap:10px;color:#6b7280;"><span class="loading-spinner"></span> Loading sessions…</div>';
   755→  let activeSessions = [];
   756→  let sessions = [];
   757→  try {
   758→    const [activeRes, savedRes] = await Promise.all([
   759→      fetch('/api/sessions/active'),
   760→      fetch('/api/sessions'),
   761→    ]);
   762→    activeSessions = activeRes.ok ? ((await activeRes.json()).sessions || []) : [];
   763→    const savedData = savedRes.ok ? parseSessionListResponse(await savedRes.json()) : { sessions: [] };
   764→    sessions = savedData.sessions || [];
   765→  } catch (e) {
   766→    body.innerHTML = `<p style="padding:20px;color:#ef4444;">Could not load sessions: ${escapeHtml(e.message)}</p>`;
   767→    return;
   768→  }
   769→  const html = _renderSessionSwitcherSections(activeSessions, sessions, { includeSavedManagement: true });
   770→  // Wrap in a single-use div so listeners are destroyed when the div is replaced on the next render
   771→  const smWrapper = document.createElement('div');
   772→  smWrapper.innerHTML = html;
   773→  body.innerHTML = '';
   774→  body.appendChild(smWrapper);
   775→  // Wire up session-modal action buttons via event delegation (avoids inline onclick with path data)
   776→  smWrapper.addEventListener('click', e => {
   777→    const btn = e.target.closest('[data-sm-action]');
   778→    if (!btn) return;
   779→    const path   = btn.dataset.smPath;
   780→    const idx    = parseInt(btn.dataset.smIdx ?? '-1', 10);
   781→    const action = btn.dataset.smAction;
   782→    if      (action === 'rename')        startSessionModalRename(path, idx);
   783→    else if (action === 'submit-rename') submitSessionModalRename(path, idx);
   784→    else if (action === 'cancel-rename') cancelSessionModalRename(idx);
   785→    else if (action === 'load')          loadSessionAndCloseModal(path);
   786→    else if (action === 'delete')        _deleteSessionFromModal(path, e);
   787→  });
   788→  smWrapper.querySelectorAll('.sm-key-input').forEach(input => {
   789→    input.addEventListener('keydown', e => {
   790→      const path = input.dataset.smPath;
   791→      const idx  = parseInt(input.dataset.smIdx, 10);
   792→      if (e.key === 'Enter') submitSessionModalRename(path, idx);
   793→      else if (e.key === 'Escape') cancelSessionModalRename(idx);
   794→    });
   795→  });
   796→}
   797→
   798→async function loadSessionAndCloseModal(path) {
   799→  closeSessionsModal();
   800→  await loadSessionFile(path);
   801→}
   802→
   803→async function newSessionFromModal() {
   804→  closeSessionsModal();
   805→  await createNewSessionAndNavigate();
   806→}
   807→
   808→function startSessionModalRename(path, idx) {
   809→  const form = document.getElementById(`sm-rename-${idx}`);
   810→  if (form) form.style.display = 'flex';
   811→  const input = document.getElementById(`sm-input-${idx}`);
   812→  if (input) { input.focus(); input.select(); }
   813→}
   814→function cancelSessionModalRename(idx) {
   815→  const form = document.getElementById(`sm-rename-${idx}`);
   816→  if (form) form.style.display = 'none';
   817→}
   818→async function submitSessionModalRename(path, idx) {
   819→  const input = document.getElementById(`sm-input-${idx}`);
   820→  if (!input) return;
   821→  const newName = input.value.trim();
   822→  if (!newName) return;
   823→  try {
   824→    const res  = await fetch('/api/rename-session', {
   825→      method: 'POST', headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
   826→      body: JSON.stringify({ path, new_name: newName }),
   827→    });
   828→    const data = await res.json();
   829→    if (data.ok) {
   830→      const nameEl = document.getElementById(`sm-name-${idx}`);
   831→      if (nameEl) nameEl.textContent = newName;
   832→      cancelSessionModalRename(idx);
   833→      await fetchStatus();
   834→    } else {
   835→      alert(`Rename failed: ${data.error}`);
   836→    }
   837→  } catch (e) { alert(`Rename error: ${e.message}`); }
   838→}
   839→
   840→async function _deleteSessionFromModal(path, event) {
   841→  event.stopPropagation();
   842→  try {
   843→    const res  = await fetch('/api/delete-session', {
   844→      method: 'POST', headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
   845→      body: JSON.stringify({ path }),
   846→    });
   847→    const data = await res.json();
   848→    if (data.success) {
   849→      await _renderSessionsModalBody();
   850→      await _refreshTrashBadge();
   851→    } else {
   852→      alert(`Failed to move session to Trash: ${data.error || 'Unknown error'}`);
   853→    }
   854→  } catch (e) { alert(`Error: ${e.message}`); }
   855→}
   856→
   857→// ── Trash badge ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   858→async function _refreshTrashBadge() {
   859→  const btn = document.getElementById('sessions-trash-btn');
   860→  if (!btn) return;
   861→  try {
   862→    const res  = await fetch('/api/trash');
   863→    const data = await res.json();
   864→    const n = (data.items || []).length;
   865→    btn.textContent = n > 0 ? `🗑 Trash (${n})` : '🗑 Trash';
   866→  } catch (_) { /* ignore */ }
   867→}
   868→
   869→// ── Trash view (inline in sessions modal) ────────────────────────────────────
   870→async function openTrashView() {
   871→  const body   = document.getElementById('sessions-modal-body');
   872→  const footer = document.querySelector('#sessions-modal-overlay .modal-footer');
   873→  if (!body) return;
   874→
   875→  body.innerHTML = '<p style="padding:24px;text-align:center;color:#6b7280;">Loading trash…</p>';
   876→
   877→  // Swap footer buttons
   878→  if (footer) {
   879→    footer.innerHTML = `
   880→      <button class="action-btn" onclick="emptyTrash()"
   881→        style="background:#fee2e2;color:#dc2626;border-color:#fecaca;">Empty Trash</button>
   882→      <button class="action-btn" onclick="closeTrashView()">← Back</button>`;
   883→  }
   884→
   885→  await _renderTrashView();
   886→}
   887→
   888→function closeTrashView() {
   889→  const footer = document.querySelector('#sessions-modal-overlay .modal-footer');
   890→  if (footer) {
   891→    footer.innerHTML = `
   892→      <button class="action-btn" onclick="newSessionFromModal()"
   893→        style="background:#10b981;color:#fff;border-color:#10b981;">＋ New Session</button>
   894→      <div style="display:flex;gap:8px;align-items:center;">
   895→        <button id="sessions-trash-btn" class="action-btn"
   896→          onclick="openTrashView()"
   897→          style="background:#f1f5f9;color:#64748b;border-color:#e2e8f0;">🗑 Trash</button>
   898→        <button class="action-btn" onclick="closeSessionsModal()">Close</button>
   899→      </div>`;
   900→  }
   901→  _renderSessionsModalBody();
   902→  _refreshTrashBadge();
   903→}
   904→
   905→async function _renderTrashView() {
   906→  const body = document.getElementById('sessions-modal-body');
   907→  if (!body) return;
   908→  let items = [];
   909→  try {
   910→    const res  = await fetch('/api/trash');
   911→    const data = await res.json();
   912→    items = data.items || [];
   913→  } catch (e) {
   914→    body.innerHTML = `<p style="padding:20px;color:#ef4444;">Could not load trash: ${escapeHtml(e.message)}</p>`;
   915→    return;
   916→  }
   917→  if (items.length === 0) {
   918→    body.innerHTML = '<p style="padding:24px;text-align:center;color:#6b7280;">Trash is empty.</p>';
   919→    return;
   920→  }
   921→
   922→  function fmtDate(ts) {
   923→    if (!ts) return '';
   924→    try { return new Date(ts).toLocaleDateString('en-US', { month: 'short', day: 'numeric', year: 'numeric' }); }
   925→    catch { return ts.slice(0, 10); }
   926→  }
   927→
   928→  let html = '<table style="width:100%;border-collapse:collapse;">';
   929→  items.forEach(s => {
   930→    const ep = escapeHtml(s.path); // HTML-safe for attributes; dataset read-back is decoded by browser
   931→    html += `
   932→      <tr style="border-bottom:1px solid #f1f5f9;vertical-align:top;">
   933→        <td style="padding:12px 16px;">
   934→          <div style="font-weight:600;color:#1e293b;">${escapeHtml(s.position_name || 'Untitled')}</div>
   935→          <div style="font-size:12px;color:#94a3b8;margin-top:2px;">${fmtDate(s.timestamp)}${s.phase ? ' · ' + escapeHtml(s.phase) : ''}</div>
   936→        </td>
   937→        <td style="padding:12px 16px;white-space:nowrap;vertical-align:middle;">
   938→          <div style="display:flex;gap:6px;">
   939→            <button data-trash-action="restore" data-trash-path="${ep}"
   940→              style="background:#dbeafe;color:#1d4ed8;border:1px solid #bfdbfe;border-radius:6px;padding:6px 12px;font-size:13px;font-weight:600;cursor:pointer;">Restore</button>
   941→            <button data-trash-action="delete-forever" data-trash-path="${ep}"
   942→              style="background:#fee2e2;color:#dc2626;border:1px solid #fecaca;border-radius:6px;padding:6px 8px;font-size:13px;cursor:pointer;" title="Delete permanently">✕</button>
   943→          </div>
   944→        </td>
   945→      </tr>`;
   946→  });
   947→  html += '</table>';
   948→  // Wrap in a single-use div so listeners are destroyed when the div is replaced on the next render
   949→  const trashWrapper = document.createElement('div');
   950→  trashWrapper.innerHTML = html;
   951→  body.innerHTML = '';
   952→  body.appendChild(trashWrapper);
   953→  // Wire up trash action buttons via event delegation
   954→  trashWrapper.addEventListener('click', e => {
   955→    const btn = e.target.closest('[data-trash-action]');
   956→    if (!btn) return;
   957→    const path   = btn.dataset.trashPath;
   958→    const action = btn.dataset.trashAction;
   959→    if      (action === 'restore')        restoreFromTrash(path);
   960→    else if (action === 'delete-forever') deleteForever(path);
   961→  });
   962→}
   963→
   964→async function restoreFromTrash(path) {
   965→  try {
   966→    const res  = await fetch('/api/trash/restore', {
   967→      method: 'POST', headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
   968→      body: JSON.stringify({ path }),
   969→    });
   970→    const data = await res.json();
   971→    if (data.success) { await _renderTrashView(); await _refreshTrashBadge(); }
   972→    else alert(`Restore failed: ${data.error || 'Unknown error'}`);
   973→  } catch (e) { alert(`Error: ${e.message}`); }
   974→}
   975→
   976→async function deleteForever(path) {
   977→  const confirmed = await confirmDialog(
   978→    'Permanently delete this session? This cannot be undone.',
   979→    { confirmLabel: 'Delete Forever', cancelLabel: 'Cancel', danger: true }
   980→  );
   981→  if (!confirmed) return;
   982→  try {
   983→    const res  = await fetch('/api/trash/delete', {
   984→      method: 'POST', headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
   985→      body: JSON.stringify({ path }),
   986→    });
   987→    const data = await res.json();
   988→    if (data.success) { await _renderTrashView(); await _refreshTrashBadge(); }
   989→    else alert(`Delete failed: ${data.error || 'Unknown error'}`);
   990→  } catch (e) { alert(`Error: ${e.message}`); }
   991→}
   992→
   993→async function emptyTrash() {
   994→  const confirmed = await confirmDialog(
   995→    'Permanently delete all items in Trash? This cannot be undone.',
   996→    { confirmLabel: 'Empty Trash', cancelLabel: 'Cancel', danger: true }
   997→  );
   998→  if (!confirmed) return;
   999→  try {
  1000→    const res  = await fetch('/api/trash/empty', { method: 'POST' });
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
     1→/**
     2→ * layout-instruction.js
     3→ * Frontend UI for natural-language layout instruction workflow.
     4→ * Handles instruction submission, preview updates, and instruction history.
     5→ *
     6→ * DEPENDENCIES:
     7→ * - utils.js (for htmlEscape function)
     8→ * - api-client.js (for apiCall function)
     9→ */
    10→
    11→/**
    12→ * Initialize layout instruction UI and event handlers.
    13→ * Called when layout tab is activated.
    14→ */
    15→function initiateLayoutInstructions() {
    16→  const instructionTab = document.getElementById('tab-layout');
    17→  if (!instructionTab) return;
    18→
    19→  // Create two-column layout if it doesn't exist
    20→  if (!instructionTab.querySelector('.layout-instruction-panel')) {
    21→    instructionTab.innerHTML = `
    22→      <div class="layout-instruction-panel">
    23→        <div class="layout-preview-pane">
    24→          <h3>Current Layout Preview</h3>
    25→          <div class="preview-iframe-container">
    26→            <iframe id="layout-preview" class="layout-preview-iframe" title="CV Layout Preview"></iframe>
    27→          </div>
    28→        </div>
    29→
    30→        <div class="layout-input-pane">
    31→          <h3>Layout Instructions</h3>
    32→          <p class="layout-scope-label">💡 Layout changes only — approved text is never modified</p>
    33→
    34→          <textarea
    35→            id="instruction-input"
    36→            class="layout-instruction-textarea"
    37→            placeholder="e.g., Move Publications section after Skills&#10;or: Make the Summary section smaller&#10;or: Keep the Genentech entry on one page"
    38→            rows="8"></textarea>
    39→
    40→          <button id="apply-instruction-btn" class="btn btn-primary layout-action-btn">
    41→            Apply Instruction
    42→          </button>
    43→
    44→          <div id="processing-indicator" class="processing-indicator" style="display: none;">
    45→            <div class="spinner"></div>
    46→            <p>Applying instruction...</p>
    47→          </div>
    48→
    49→          <div id="confirmation-message" class="confirmation-message" style="display: none;"></div>
    50→
    51→          <div class="layout-history-section">
    52→            <h4>
    53→              <span class="history-toggle">▼</span>
    54→              Instruction History (<span id="instruction-count">0</span>)
    55→            </h4>
    56→            <div id="instruction-history" class="instruction-history-list"></div>
    57→          </div>
    58→
    59→          <button id="proceed-to-finalise-btn" class="btn btn-success layout-action-btn" style="display: none;">
    60→            Proceed to Final Generation
    61→          </button>
    62→        </div>
    63→      </div>
    64→    `;
    65→
    66→    // Wire up event listeners
    67→    setupLayoutInstructionListeners();
    68→  }
    69→
    70→  // Load and display current HTML preview
    71→  const currentHtml = window.tabData?.cv?.['*.html'] || '';
    72→  if (currentHtml) {
    73→    displayLayoutPreview(currentHtml);
    74→  }
    75→
    76→  // Restore any prior instructions from session
    77→  restoreInstructionHistory();
    78→}
    79→
    80→/**
    81→ * Set up event listeners for layout instruction UI.
    82→ */
    83→function setupLayoutInstructionListeners() {
    84→  const applyBtn = document.getElementById('apply-instruction-btn');
    85→  const proceedBtn = document.getElementById('proceed-to-finalise-btn');
    86→  const instructionInput = document.getElementById('instruction-input');
    87→  const historyToggle = document.querySelector('.history-toggle');
    88→
    89→  if (applyBtn) {
    90→    applyBtn.addEventListener('click', () => {
    91→      const instruction = instructionInput.value.trim();
    92→      if (!instruction) {
    93→        appendMessage('system', '⚠️ Please enter a layout instruction before submitting.');
    94→        return;
    95→      }
    96→      submitLayoutInstruction(instruction);
    97→    });
    98→  }
    99→
   100→  if (proceedBtn) {
   101→    proceedBtn.addEventListener('click', completeLayoutReview);
   102→  }
   103→
   104→  if (historyToggle) {
   105→    historyToggle.addEventListener('click', (e) => {
   106→      e.target.textContent = e.target.textContent === '▼' ? '▶' : '▼';
   107→      const historyList = document.getElementById('instruction-history');
   108→      if (historyList) {
   109→        historyList.classList.toggle('collapsed');
   110→      }
   111→    });
   112→  }
   113→
   114→  // Allow Enter key to submit in textarea (Shift+Enter for new line)
   115→  if (instructionInput) {
   116→    instructionInput.addEventListener('keypress', (e) => {
   117→      if (e.key === 'Enter' && !e.shiftKey) {
   118→        e.preventDefault();
   119→        applyBtn?.click();
   120→      }
   121→    });
   122→  }
   123→}
   124→
   125→/**
   126→ * Submit layout instruction to backend for processing.
   127→ */
   128→async function submitLayoutInstruction(instructionText) {
   129→  const currentHtml = window.tabData?.cv?.['*.html'] || '';
   130→  const priorInstructions = window.layoutInstructions || [];
   131→
   132→  try {
   133→    showProcessing(true);
   134→
   135→    const response = await apiCall('POST', '/api/layout-instruction', {
   136→      instruction: instructionText,
   137→      current_html: currentHtml,
   138→      prior_instructions: priorInstructions
   139→    });
   140→
   141→    if (!response.ok) {
   142→      if (response.error === 'clarify') {
   143→        showClarificationDialog(response.question, instructionText);
   144→      } else {
   145→        appendMessage('system', `⚠️ Error: ${response.error} — ${response.details || ''}`);
   146→      }
   147→      return;
   148→    }
   149→
   150→    // Update preview with new HTML
   151→    const newHtml = response.html;
   152→    displayLayoutPreview(newHtml);
   153→
   154→    // Update state
   155→    window.tabData.cv['*.html'] = newHtml;
   156→
   157→    // Add to instruction history
   158→    const instruction = {
   159→      timestamp: new Date().toLocaleTimeString(),
   160→      instruction_text: instructionText,
   161→      change_summary: response.summary,
   162→      confirmation: true
   163→    };
   164→    addToInstructionHistory(instruction);
   165→
   166→    // Show confirmation
   167→    showConfirmationMessage(`✅ ${response.summary}`);
   168→
   169→    // Clear input and show proceed button
   170→    document.getElementById('instruction-input').value = '';
   171→    document.getElementById('proceed-to-finalise-btn').style.display = 'block';
   172→
   173→  } catch (error) {
   174→    appendMessage('system', `❌ Failed to apply layout instruction: ${error.message}`);
   175→  } finally {
   176→    showProcessing(false);
   177→  }
   178→}
   179→
   180→/**
   181→ * Display HTML preview in iframe.
   182→ */
   183→function displayLayoutPreview(html) {
   184→  const preview = document.getElementById('layout-preview');
   185→  if (!preview) return;
   186→
   187→  // Set iframe content safely
   188→  const doc = preview.contentDocument || preview.contentWindow?.document;
   189→  if (doc) {
   190→    doc.open();
   191→    doc.write(html);
   192→    doc.close();
   193→  }
   194→}
   195→
   196→/**
   197→ * Add instruction to history panel.
   198→ */
   199→function addToInstructionHistory(instruction) {
   200→  // Initialize global instruction list if needed
   201→  if (!window.layoutInstructions) {
   202→    window.layoutInstructions = [];
   203→  }
   204→
   205→  window.layoutInstructions.push(instruction);
   206→
   207→  const historyList = document.getElementById('instruction-history');
   208→  if (!historyList) return;
   209→
   210→  const entry = document.createElement('div');
   211→  entry.className = 'instruction-history-entry';
   212→  entry.innerHTML = `
   213→    <div class="instruction-time">${instruction.timestamp}</div>
   214→    <div class="instruction-text">${htmlEscape(instruction.instruction_text)}</div>
   215→    <div class="instruction-summary"><em>${instruction.change_summary}</em></div>
   216→    <button class="btn btn-small" onclick="undoInstruction(${window.layoutInstructions.length - 1})">
   217→      Undo
   218→    </button>
   219→  `;
   220→
   221→  historyList.appendChild(entry);
   222→
   223→  // Update count
   224→  document.getElementById('instruction-count').textContent = window.layoutInstructions.length;
   225→}
   226→
   227→/**
   228→ * Restore instruction history from session state.
   229→ */
   230→function restoreInstructionHistory() {
   231→  const instructions = window.layoutInstructions || [];
   232→  instructions.forEach(addToInstructionHistory);
   233→
   234→  // Show proceed button if any instructions applied
   235→  if (instructions.length > 0) {
   236→    document.getElementById('proceed-to-finalise-btn').style.display = 'block';
   237→  }
   238→}
   239→
   240→/**
   241→ * Show processing spinner.
   242→ */
   243→function showProcessing(show) {
   244→  const indicator = document.getElementById('processing-indicator');
   245→  if (indicator) {
   246→    indicator.style.display = show ? 'block' : 'none';
   247→  }
   248→}
   249→
   250→/**
   251→ * Show confirmation message.
   252→ */
   253→function showConfirmationMessage(message) {
   254→  const element = document.getElementById('confirmation-message');
   255→  if (!element) return;
   256→
   257→  element.textContent = message;
   258→  element.style.display = 'block';
   259→
   260→  // Auto-hide after 3 seconds
   261→  setTimeout(() => {
   262→    element.style.display = 'none';
   263→  }, 3000);
   264→}
   265→
   266→/**
   267→ * Show inline clarification dialog when LLM needs more info.
   268→ */
   269→function showClarificationDialog(question, originalInstruction) {
   270→  const response = prompt(
   271→    `The system needs clarification:\n\n${question}\n\nYour original: "${originalInstruction}"\n\nPlease clarify:`,
   272→    originalInstruction
   273→  );
   274→
   275→  if (response && response !== originalInstruction) {
   276→    submitLayoutInstruction(response);
   277→  }
   278→}
   279→
   280→/**
   281→ * Undo a specific instruction (regenerate from prior step).
   282→ */
   283→function undoInstruction(index) {
   284→  if (!window.layoutInstructions || index < 0 || index >= window.layoutInstructions.length) {
   285→    return;
   286→  }
   287→
   288→  window.layoutInstructions.splice(index, 1);
   289→
   290→  // Regenerate preview from HTML at this point
   291→  // (simplified: in production, would re-apply all prior instructions)
   292→  appendMessage('system', '🔄 Undo not yet implemented — would regenerate from prior state');
   293→
   294→  // Update history display
   295→  const historyList = document.getElementById('instruction-history');
   296→  if (historyList) {
   297→    historyList.innerHTML = '';
   298→    window.layoutInstructions.forEach(addToInstructionHistory);
   299→  }
   300→}
   301→
   302→/**
   303→ * Complete layout review and advance to finalise phase.
   304→ */
   305→async function completeLayoutReview() {
   306→  try {
   307→    showProcessing(true);
   308→
   309→    const response = await apiCall('POST', '/api/layout-complete', {
   310→      layout_instructions: window.layoutInstructions || []
   311→    });
   312→
   313→    if (!response.ok) {
   314→      appendMessage('system', `❌ Error: ${response.error}`);
   315→      return;
   316→    }
   317→
   318→    appendMessage('assistant', '✅ Layout confirmed. Ready to generate final output.');
   319→
   320→    // Update phase and switch to download/generation tab
   321→    stateManager.setPhase('refinement');
   322→    switchTab('download');
   323→
   324→  } catch (error) {
   325→    appendMessage('system', `❌ Failed to complete layout review: ${error.message}`);
   326→  } finally {
   327→    showProcessing(false);
   328→  }
   329→}
   330→
```

<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./tasks/user-story-resume-expert.md`

```text
     1→# User Story: Resume Optimisation Expert Perspective
     2→**Persona:** A certified professional résumé writer / career strategist evaluating the quality, strategy, and correctness of the system's output  
     3→**Scope:** Evaluating each stage where the system makes content or presentation decisions  
     4→**Format:** Evaluation criteria presented as acceptance tests, with specific failure modes to guard against
     5→
     6→---
     7→
     8→## US-R1: Job Description Analysis Quality
     9→
    10→**As a** resume optimisation expert,  
    11→**I want to** verify that the system correctly classifies required vs. preferred qualifications and extracts the right keywords,  
    12→**So that** the candidate's CV targets the right content and achieves maximum keyword density without overfitting.
    13→
    14→**Evaluation Criteria:**
    15→1. **Required vs. preferred split** — The system must distinguish "required" (MUST, required, essential) from "preferred" (preferred, a plus, nice to have). Conflating them leads to over-engineering the CV for nice-to-haves.
    16→2. **Keyword deduplication** — Synonyms ("ML" and "Machine Learning") should be grouped, not counted as separate keywords.
    17→3. **Domain inference accuracy** — The inferred role type (IC vs. leadership, domain focus) should match the actual job. A false "leadership" inference causes wrong content to be emphasised.
    18→4. **Keyword frequency weighting** — Keywords appearing in the job title, first paragraph, or multiple times should be weighted higher than single-mention keywords.
    19→
    20→**Failure Modes to Guard Against:**
    21→- Treating preferred qualifications as must-haves → over-tailoring, losing breadth.
    22→- Missing implicit requirements (e.g., "cross-functional team" implies stakeholder communication skills).
    23→- Treating duplicated keywords (ML / machine learning / artificial intelligence) as three separate gaps.
    24→
    25→**Acceptance Criteria:**
    26→- Required and preferred qualifications displayed in visually distinct sections.
    27→- Synonyms and acronym/expansion pairs grouped (e.g., "ML" ≡ "Machine Learning").
    28→- Domain inference presented with confidence level; ambiguous cases prompt the user.
    29→
    30→---
    31→
    32→## US-R2: Content Selection Strategy
    33→
    34→**As a** resume optimisation expert,  
    35→**I want to** confirm that the system selects and orders content to maximise relevance to the target job while preserving the candidate's strongest material,  
    36→**So that** the CV is not just keyword-optimised but strategically compelling.
    37→
    38→**Evaluation Criteria:**
    39→1. **Recency bias check** — Most-recent experience should not be automatically highest-scored if an older role is more relevant. Relevance score should be role-based, not recency-biased.
    40→2. **Achievement ordering within a job** — The most job-relevant bullet should be first; the system must not preserve the original document order blindly.
    41→3. **Section inclusion logic** — Publications should be included for research roles, excluded for industry roles. The system should justify conditional inclusions/exclusions. For research roles, the LLM must rank publications by relevance to the specific job (keyword overlap with `ats_keywords` and `required_skills`, domain alignment, first-author status, recency) and recommend a shortlist — not simply include all or include none.
    42→4. **Publication selection quality** — The recommended publications shortlist should surface items that directly evidence the required skills and domain expertise, not the most-cited or most-recent regardless of relevance.
    43→5. **Completeness without bloat** — For a senior candidate, the CV should be 2–3 pages. The system must not pad with marginally relevant content to fill pages, nor truncate critical content to fit.
    44→6. **Selected Achievements quality** — The 4–6 selected achievements should represent diverse impact types (technical, leadership, business) appropriate to the role, not all from one domain.
    45→
    46→**Failure Modes to Guard Against:**
    47→- Recommending all experiences regardless of relevance (lazy inclusion).
    48→- Always using original bullet order (missing reordering opportunity).
    49→- Dropping Publications silently for a research role because they aren't scored highly by keyword matching.
    50→- Including all publications indiscriminately for any research role, without filtering for relevance to this specific job.
    51→
    52→**Acceptance Criteria:**
    53→- Relevance score is based on semantic + keyword match, not recency rank.
    54→- Bullet reordering is proposed and applied within each experience entry.
    55→- Conditional section decisions (Publications, Languages, Awards) are shown with rationale.
    56→- For any role where publications may be relevant, a ranked publication shortlist is presented with per-item relevance scores and rationale; it is never silently omitted or silently included in full.
    57→- System warns if estimated CV length exceeds 3 pages or is under 1.5 pages.
    58→
    59→---
    60→
    61→## US-R3: Rewrite Quality and Constraint Adherence
    62→
    63→**As a** resume optimisation expert,  
    64→**I want to** verify that every proposed text rewrite substitutes terminology naturally, preserves all factual content, and reads like a human wrote it —  
    65→**So that** the final CV is both ATS-optimised and credible to human reviewers.
    66→
    67→**Evaluation Criteria:**
    68→1. **Factual preservation** — Dates, company names, metrics (percentages, dollar figures, headcount) must be identical in the proposed text. The constraint validation (`apply_rewrite_constraints`) must catch violations.
    69→2. **Naturalness** — Rewrites should not read as keyword-stuffed or robotic. "Leveraged synergistic ML/AI methodologies" is a failure. "Built ML models" → "Built ML/AI models" is a success.
    70→3. **Keyword integration** — The job's terminology appears naturally in the sentence, not tacked onto the end. "…pipelines. MLOps." is a failure; "…MLOps pipeline deployment" is a success.
    71→4. **No fabrication** — `skill_add` proposals must cite concrete evidence from experience entries. Weak-evidence additions must be flagged for user confirmation.
    72→5. **Terminology consistency** — If "MLOps" is adopted for one bullet, the summary rewrite should also use "MLOps", not "productionizing ML pipelines".
    73→6. **Acronym expansion** — Introduced keywords should include both forms on first use: "MLOps (ML Operations)".
    74→
    75→**Failure Modes to Guard Against:**
    76→- Removing a metric during a summary rewrite (e.g., "led team of 15" becoming "led a team").
    77→- Adding a keyword at the end of a sentence as an appendage rather than substituting inline.
    78→- `skill_add` without evidence (hallucinated skills).
    79→- Inconsistent terminology across summary, bullets, and skills section.
    80→
    81→**Acceptance Criteria:**
    82→- `apply_rewrite_constraints` rejects any proposal that removes a number, date, or company name.
    83→- Every `skill_add` proposal cites at least one experience ID as evidence.
    84→- Inserted keywords appear mid-sentence, not appended.
    85→- System enforces that introduced keywords are consistent across all rewrites in a batch.
    86→
    87→---
    88→
    89→## US-R4: Professional Summary Effectiveness
    90→
    91→**As a** resume optimisation expert,  
    92→**I want to** evaluate whether the proposed professional summary rewrite is strategically strong —  
    93→**So that** the 4–6 line summary acts as a high-impact executive pitch rather than a keyword dump.
    94→
    95→**Evaluation Criteria:**
    96→1. **Hook quality** — The opening line should identify role type, years of experience, and a differentiated strength.
    97→2. **Keyword coverage** — Top 3–5 required keywords embedded naturally, not literally copied from the job posting.
    98→3. **No fluff** — Phrases like "results-driven professional" or "passionate about" are red flags.
    99→4. **Leadership scope stated** — For senior/leadership roles, the summary should mention organisational impact (team size, budget, scope).
   100→5. **Length** — 4–6 lines; not a paragraph block.
   101→
   102→**Failure Modes to Guard Against:**
   103→- Using the same summary for all roles (no customisation at all).
   104→- Opening with name or title ("Gregory Warnes is a…").
   105→- Listing keywords as a comma-separated sentence ("Expertise in: Python, ML, R…").
   106→
   107→**Acceptance Criteria:**
   108→- Proposed summary is role-specific (different from any stored `professional_summaries` variant unless a good match exists).
   109→- Opening sentence is evaluable: contains role type + years experience + differentiator.
   110→- System does not inject the phrase "results-driven" or similar filler.
   111→
   112→---
   113→
   114→## US-R5: Skills Section Optimisation
   115→
   116→**As a** resume optimisation expert,  
   117→**I want to** verify that the skills section accurately represents the candidate's demonstrated abilities in job-relevant terminology —  
   118→**So that** ATS keyword scanning succeeds and the skills section adds credibility, not noise.
   119→
   120→**Evaluation Criteria:**
   121→1. **Terminology alignment** — Skill display names should use the job posting's phrasing where the underlying skill is equivalent.
   122→2. **No fabrication** — Skills the candidate cannot demonstrate should never appear, even if the job requires them.
   123→3. **Grouping logic** — Skills should be categorised sensibly for the role type (e.g., "Infrastructure & Cloud" de-emphasised for a pure research role).
   124→4. **Density without redundancy** — Listing "Python", "Python 3", "Python (pandas, scikit-learn)" as three separate entries is redundant. One canonical form with parenthetical aliases.
   125→5. **"Candidate to confirm" handling** — Weak-evidence additions must be visually flagged; they should not slip through, but they also should not be silently dropped.
   126→
   127→**Failure Modes to Guard Against:**
   128→- Renaming a skill to something the candidate has not done ("Container Orchestration" renamed to "Container Orchestration (Kubernetes)" when candidate has never used Kubernetes).
   129→- All skills listed alphabetically with no role-relevance ordering.
   130→- Omitting high-relevance skills because they fall in a de-emphasised category.
   131→
   132→**Acceptance Criteria:**
   133→- Only `skills` entries present in `Master_CV_Data.json` (or explicitly approved additions) appear in the output.
   134→- Skills ordered by relevance to the target role within each category group.
   135→- Any approved additional skills are added to `Master_CV_Data.json` for future use, with relevant experience.
   136→- Candidate-to-confirm items are clearly flagged in the skills review UI (e.g., asterisk, footnote, or distinct visual indicator). They must **never appear in generated output documents** — all generated PDF, DOCX, and HTML files must contain only clean, unmarked text. The marking is a review-step affordance only.
   137→
   138→---
   139→
   140→## US-R6: Rewrite Audit Traceability
   141→
   142→**As a** resume optimisation expert,  
   143→**I want to** review the complete audit trail of proposed, accepted, edited, and rejected rewrites —  
   144→**So that** I can verify that every word in the final CV is traceable to either an approved rewrite or the original master data.
   145→
   146→**Evaluation Criteria:**
   147→1. **Full traceability** — Every field in the generated CV should be traceable to either: (a) the original `Master_CV_Data.json` value, or (b) a user-approved rewrite in `rewrite_audit`.
   148→2. **Rejected rewrites reverted** — If a rewrite is rejected, the original text—not the proposed text—must appear in the output.
   149→3. **Edited rewrites** — If a rewrite is edited, the user's final text—not the LLM's proposal—must appear.
   150→4. **Audit completeness** — `rewrite_audit` must include all proposals, not just accepted ones.
   151→
   152→**Acceptance Criteria:**
   153→- `rewrite_audit` in `metadata.json` contains an entry for every proposal, with `outcome: accept | reject | edit` and `final` text.
   154→- Diff between generated CV text and `rewrite_audit.final` values = zero unexplained changes.
   155→- Audit non-empty even when all rewrites are rejected (audit records rejections).
   156→
   157→---
   158→
   159→## US-R7: Spell & Grammar Check Quality
   160→
   161→**As a** resume optimisation expert,  
   162→**I want to** verify that the spell/grammar checker raises accurate, useful flags and suppresses irrelevant noise —  
   163→**So that** the candidate is not burdened with false positives, and genuine errors are caught reliably.
   164→
   165→**Evaluation Criteria:**
   166→
   167→1. **No false positives on technical vocabulary** — Domain terms (`MLOps`, `biostatistics`, `Bioconductor`, `WeasyPrint`, `scikit-learn`) must not be flagged when they appear in `custom_dictionary.json` or are correctly spelled recognised terms.
   168→2. **No false positives on proper nouns** — Company names (Genentech, Pfizer, Torqata), candidate name, product names — never flagged.
   169→3. **Fragment tolerance in bullets** — Bullets beginning with an action verb (`Led…`, `Designed…`, `Built…`) must not trigger sentence-fragment warnings. The system must detect `bullet` context and suppress fragment rules.
   170→4. **Skill names treated as words/phrases** — `skill_name` context receives spelling-only checking; grammar rules are not applied.
   171→5. **Corrections do not alter approved rewrite text beyond the flagged span** — Accepting a spelling correction for one word must not rewrite surrounding approved text.
   172→6. **Custom dictionary seeded correctly** — On first run, the dictionary is pre-populated from `Master_CV_Data.json` (candidate name, companies, key technical terms) so common items are never flagged.
   173→7. **Severity calibration** — Critical errors (misspelled common words) surfaced before minor stylistic suggestions; the list is sorted by severity.
   174→
   175→**Failure Modes to Guard Against:**
   176→- `MLOps` flagged as misspelling because it's not in a standard English dictionary.
   177→- Bullet `"Led team of 15 engineers across 3 time zones"` flagged as a sentence fragment.
   178→- Accepting a comma suggestion rewriting the entire sentence.
   179→- Custom dictionary growing without bound (duplicate entries, whitespace variants).
   180→- Checker runs on fields not shown to the user (internal metadata keys, JSON keys).
   181→
   182→**Acceptance Criteria:**
   183→- All terms in `custom_dictionary.json` produce zero flags, regardless of context.
   184→- A test bullet beginning with a strong action verb produces zero fragment warnings.
   185→- `skill_name` context entries produce only spelling flags, never grammar flags.
   186→- Accepted corrections change exactly and only the flagged span in the source text.
   187→- `custom_dictionary.json` is deduplicated on every write; no duplicate entries.
   188→- Spell audit in `metadata.json` (`spell_audit` array) is non-empty when flags exist and fully empty (zero entries) when no flags were found.
   189→
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
   206→          <button class="action-btn" onclick="closeSessionsModal()">Close</button>
   207→        </div>
   208→      </div>
   209→    </div>

   211→
   212→  <!-- Toast Notification -->
   213→  <div id="toast-container" aria-live="polite" aria-atomic="true"></div>
   214→
   215→  <!-- Alert Modal -->
   216→  <div class="modal-overlay" id="alert-modal-overlay" style="display: none;" role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby="alert-modal-title" aria-describedby="alert-modal-message">
   217→    <div class="modal" style="max-width: 500px;">
   218→      <div class="modal-header">

   229→
   230→  <div class="modal-overlay" id="ownership-conflict-overlay" style="display: none;" role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby="ownership-conflict-title" aria-describedby="ownership-conflict-message" onclick="if(event.target===this)closeOwnershipConflictDialog('different')">

   232→      <div class="modal-header">

   235→      <div class="modal-body">
   236→        <p id="ownership-conflict-message" style="line-height: 1.6; color: #475569;">This session is already open in another browser tab.</p>
   237→      </div>
   238→      <div class="modal-footer" style="display:flex;justify-content:flex-end;gap:8px;flex-wrap:wrap;">
   239→        <button class="action-btn" id="ownership-load-different-btn">Load Different</button>
   240→        <button class="action-btn" id="ownership-new-session-btn">New Session</button>
   241→        <button class="action-btn primary" id="ownership-takeover-btn">Take Over</button>
   242→      </div>
   243→    </div>
   244→  </div>
   245→
   246→  <!-- Model Selection Modal -->
   247→  <div class="modal-overlay" id="model-modal-overlay" style="display: none;" role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby="model-modal-title" onclick="if(event.target===this)closeModelModal()">
   248→    <div class="modal" style="max-width: 1020px; width: 95%;">
   249→      <div class="modal-header" style="display: flex; justify-content: space-between; align-items: center;">
   250→        <h2 id="model-modal-title" style="margin:0;">Select Model</h2>
   251→        <button onclick="closeModelModal()" style="background:none;border:none;font-size:1.4em;cursor:pointer;color:#64748b;" title="Close">&times;</button>
   252→      </div>
   253→      <div class="modal-body" style="padding: 0;">
   254→        <div style="padding:10px 14px; border-bottom:1px solid #e2e8f0; background:#f8fafc;">
   255→          <div style="font-size:0.85em; color:#334155; margin-bottom:6px;">
   256→            Step 1: Select provider(s)
   257→          </div>
   258→          <div id="model-provider-list" style="display:flex; flex-wrap:wrap; gap:8px;"></div>
   259→        </div>
   260→        <table id="model-table" style="width:100%; border-collapse: collapse; font-size: 0.9em;">
   261→          <thead id="model-table-head">
   262→            <tr style="background: #f1f5f9; text-align: left;">
   263→              <th style="padding: 10px 14px; white-space: nowrap;">Provider</th>

   268→              <th style="padding: 10px 14px; white-space: nowrap; text-align: right;" title="GitHub Copilot premium-request multiplier (0 = free for paid subscribers)">Copilot</th>
   269→              <th style="padding: 10px 14px; white-space: nowrap;">Source</th>

   284→            &#10003; Test connection
```
